MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-22-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER GRANTING PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT RETAIN CONTROL OVER THE OPERATIVE DETAILS OF THE WORK OR THE INSTRUMENTALITIES THAT CAUSED THE INJURY. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HIGHLIGHT ANY CONDUCT OF DEFENDANT THAT AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTED TO THE INJURY. SEE HOOKER V. DEPT. OF TRANSPORTATION, (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 198, 202. IT WAS MR. CAPITANI'S OPERATION OF THE BOBCAT THAT CAUSED THE INJURY. DEFENDANT'S GENERAL INSTRUCTION TO STORE THE EXCAVATED DEBRIS IN A CERTAIN AREA IS NOT THE TYPE OF AFFIRMATIVE CONDUCT TO CREATE LIABILITY. ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Raymond Ghermezian of Law Offices of David Azizi telephone number 310-284-9600 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Jose Garcia.
Wendy Schenk of Hardiman & Carroll, Inc. telephone number 415-248-3930 for Defendant Plant Construction Company.
Kevin Clarke of Glaspy & Glaspy telephone number 925-947-1300 appeared by telephone for Defendants Whiteside Construction Corp., Inc. and Tom Capitani.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. In re: DEFENDANT PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendant did not retain control over the operative details of the work or the instrumentalities that caused the injury. Plaintiff does not highlight any conduct of defendant that affirmatively contributed to the injury. See Hooker v. Dept. of Transportation, (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198, 202. It was Mr. Capitani's operation of the bobcat that caused the injury. Defendant's general instruction to store the excavated debris in a certain area is not the type of affirmative conduct to create liability. Accordingly, the motion for summary judgment is granted. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
